_**User**_

## 搜索目标
找到支持"多窗口/多Agent协作、任务分发、消息传递"的agent skills。

## 搜索方法

### 1. WebSearch搜索ClawHub
用WebSearch搜索以下关键词（分别搜索）：
- "clawhub multi-agent orchestration skill"
- "clawhub agent coordination task dispatch"
- "openclaw multi agent workflow"

### 2. WebSearch搜索skills.sh
- "skills.sh multi-agent orchestration"
- "skills.sh agent coordination"

### 3. WebSearch搜索GitHub
- "github SKILL.md multi-agent orchestration"
- "github agent-skills task dispatch coordination"
- "github SKILL.md agent workflow automation"

### 4. 通用搜索
- "AI agent multi-agent orchestration tool 2026"
- "cursor agent skills multi window coordination"

## 输出要求
对每个找到的候选SK，列出：
- 名称
- 来源（ClawHub/skills.sh/GitHub）
- 描述
- 星数/下载量（如有）
- 最后更新时间（如有）
- 安装方式
- GitHub URL或安装命令

尽量找3个以上候选。如果某个平台没找到，说明。

It enables task creation and scheduling, message routing between agents, role-based access control, and historical activity inspection for auditing and debugging.[4]<br/><br/>## Real-World Workflow<br/>These tools work together in complex pipelines where a Manager agent orchestrates specialized agents (Researcher, Coder, etc.), each handling specific domains while remaining coordinated through the session tools.[2] |

domain-specific knowledge that teach AI agents how to perform specific tasks.[3] They're defined in `SKILL.md` files and can include scripts, templates, and references.[3] Skills are automatically discovered from `.agents/skills/`, `.cursor/skills/`, and `~/.cursor/skills/` directories.[3][4]<br/><br/>**When to use skills:** Multi-step workflows and procedures, as opposed to rules which are short guidelines.[4] Skills can be invoked with `/skill-name` in chat or attached via `@skill-name`.[4]<br/><br/>## Multi-Agent Coordination<br/><br/>For coordinating work between multiple AI agents, the multi-agent-coordination skill provides three coordination mechanisms:[1]<br/><br/>1. **Git-Based** — Uses AGENCY.md files as shared memory with Git commits tracking state changes<br/>2. **Cortex Orchestration** — Automated coordination running every 4 hours via GitHub Actions<br/>3. **Real-Time Coordinator** — Optional FastAPI server for immediate coordination preventing simultaneous claims[1]<br/><br/>**Ownership protocol:** Agents must claim projects before working by setting `owner: agent-identifier` in AGENCY.md frontmatter, respecting one-owner-at-a-time rules.[1]<br/><br/>**Dependency management:** Define dependencies using blocked_by, blocks, parent, and related fields to manage task sequencing across projects.[1]<br/><br/>## Subagents for Parallel Coordination<br/><br/>Cursor 2.4 introduced subagents—independent agents that handle discrete subtasks in parallel, each with its own context window and tool access.[2][5] This enables:<br/><br/>- **Parallel execution** — Multiple subagents work simultaneously on different parts of the codebase[5]<br/>- **Context isolation** — Long research tasks don't consume space in the main conversation[5]<br/>- **Specialized expertise** — Configure subagents with custom prompts and models for domain-specific tasks[5]<br/><br/>Built-in subagents include Explore (codebase analysis), Bash (command execution), and Browser (web interactions).[5] |

goal.\n\n### When Multi-Agent Systems Shine\n\n- Scalability: Easy to add new specialized agents\n- Resilience: Failures in one agent don't break the entire system\n- Collaborative Reasoning: Agents debate, refine, and improve solutions\n- Parallel Processing: Multiple agents work on different aspects simultaneously\n- Domain-Specific Expertise: Finance, legal, HR, engineering need different knowledge\n- Complex Workflows: Tasks requiring multiple specialized roles\n\n### Architecture Overview\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nUser Request\n    ↓\nOrchestrator\n    ├→ Agent 1 (Specialist) → Task 1\n    ├→ Agent 2 (Specialist) → Task 2\n    ├→ Agent 3 (Specialist) → Task 3\n    ↓\nResult Aggregator\n    ↓\nFinal Response\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Core Concepts\n\n### Agent Definition\n\nAn agent is defined by:\n\n- Context: What information does it need to work effectively?\n- Tools: What capabilities can it access?\n- Expertise: What knowledge/tools does it have?\n- Goal: What should it accomplish? (e.g., \"Analyze financial risks\")\n- Role: What responsibility does it have? (e.g., \"Financial Analyst\")\n\n### Orchestration Patterns\n\n#### 1. Sequential Orchestration\n\n- Use Case: Steps must follow order (research → analysis → writing)\n- Each agent uses output from previous agent\n- Agents work one after another\n\n#### 2. Parallel Orchestration\n\n- Use Case: Independent tasks (analyze competitors, market, users)\n- Results aggregated at the end\n- Multiple agents work simultaneously\n\n#### 3. Hierarchical Orchestration\n\n- Use Case: Large projects with oversight\n- Manager coordinates flow\n- Senior agent delegates to junior agents\n\n#### 4. Consensus-Based Orchestration\n\n- Use Case: Complex decisions needing multiple perspectives\n- Vote or reach consensus\n- Debate and refine ideas\n- Multiple agents analyze problem\n\n#### 5. Tool-Mediated Orchestration\n\n- Use Case: Large systems, indirect coordination\n- Minimal direct communication\n- Agents use shared tools/databases\n\n## Multi-Agent Team Examples\n\n### Finance Team\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nCoordinator Agent\n    ├→ Market Analyst Agent\n    │   ├ Tools: Market data API, financial news\n    │   └ Task: Analyze market conditions\n    ├→ Financial Analyst Agent\n    │   ├ Tools: Financial statements, ratio calculations\n    │   └ Task: Analyze company financials\n    ├→ Risk Manager Agent\n    │   ├ Tools: Risk models, scenario analysis\n    │   └ Task: Assess investment risks\n    └→ Report Writer Agent\n        ├ Tools: Document generation\n        └ Task: Synthesize findings into report\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Legal Team\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nCase Manager Agent (Coordinator)\n    ├→ Contract Analyzer Agent\n    │   └ Task: Review contract terms\n    ├→ Precedent Research Agent\n    │   └ Task: Find relevant case law\n    ├→ Risk Assessor Agent\n    │   └ Task: Identify legal risks\n    └→ Document Drafter Agent\n        └ Task: Prepare legal documents\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Customer Support Team\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nSupport Coordinator\n    ├→ Issue Classifier Agent\n    │   └ Task: Categorize customer issue\n    ├→ Knowledge Base Agent\n    │   └ Task: Find relevant documentation\n    ├→ Escalation Agent\n    │   └ Task: Determine if human escalation needed\n    └→ Solution Synthesizer Agent\n        └ Task: Prepare comprehensive response\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Implementation Frameworks\n\n### 1. CrewAI\n\nBest For: Teams with clear roles and hierarchical structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom crewai import Agent, Task, Crew\n\n# Define agents\nanalyst = Agent(\n    role=\"Financial Analyst\",\n    goal=\"Analyze financial data and provide insights\",\n    backstory=\"Expert in financial markets with 10+ years experience\"\n)\n\nresearcher = Agent(\n    role=\"Market Researcher\",\n    goal=\"Research market trends and competition\",\n    backstory=\"Data-driven researcher specializing in market analysis\"\n)\n\n# Define tasks\nanalysis_task = Task(\n    description=\"Analyze Q3 financial results for {company}\",\n    agent=analyst,\n    tools=[financial_tool, data_tool]\n)\n\nresearch_task = Task(\n    description=\"Research competitive landscape in {market}\",\n    agent=researcher,\n    tools=[web_search_tool, industry_data_tool]\n)\n\n# Create crew and execute\ncrew = Crew(\n    agents=[analyst, researcher],\n    tasks=[analysis_task, research_task],\n    process=Process.sequential\n)\n\nresult = crew.kickoff(inputs={\"company\": \"TechCorp\", \"market\": \"AI\"})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. AutoGen (Microsoft)\n\nBest For: Complex multi-turn conversations and negotiations\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager\n\n# Define agents\nanalyst = AssistantAgent(\n    name=\"analyst\",\n    system_message=\"You are a financial analyst...\"\n)\n\nresearcher = AssistantAgent(\n    name=\"researcher\",\n    system_message=\"You are a market researcher...\"\n)\n\n# Create group chat\ngroupchat = GroupChat(\n    agents=[analyst, researcher],\n    messages=[],\n    max_round=10,\n    speaker_selection_method=\"auto\"\n)\n\n# Manage group conversation\nmanager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat)\n\n# User proxy to initiate conversation\nuser = UserProxyAgent(name=\"user\")\n\n# Have conversation\nuser.initiate_chat(\n    manager,\n    message=\"Analyze if Company X should invest in Y market\"\n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 3. LangGraph\n\nBest For: Complex workflows with state management\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom langgraph.graph import Graph, StateGraph\nfrom langgraph.prebuilt import create_agent_executor\n\n# Define state\nclass AgentState:\n    research_findings: str\n    analysis: str\n    recommendations: str\n\n# Create graph\ngraph = StateGraph(AgentState)\n\n# Add nodes for each agent\ngraph.add_node(\"researcher\", research_agent)\ngraph.add_node(\"analyst\", analyst_agent)\ngraph.add_node(\"writer\", writer_agent)\n\n# Define edges (workflow)\ngraph.add_edge(\"researcher\", \"analyst\")\ngraph.add_edge(\"analyst\", \"writer\")\n\n# Set entry/exit points\ngraph.set_entry_point(\"researcher\")\ngraph.set_finish_point(\"writer\")\n\n# Compile and run\nworkflow = graph.compile()\nresult = workflow.invoke({\"topic\": \"AI trends\"})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 4. OpenAI Swarm\n\nBest For: Simple agent handoffs and conversational workflows\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom swarm import Agent, Swarm\n\n# Define agents\ntriage_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"Triage Agent\",\n    instructions=\"Determine which specialist to route the customer to\"\n)\n\nbilling_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"Billing Specialist\",\n    instructions=\"Handle billing and payment questions\"\n)\n\ntechnical_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"Technical Support\",\n    instructions=\"Handle technical issues\"\n)\n\n# Define handoff functions\ndef route_to_billing(reason: str):\n    return billing_agent\n\ndef route_to_technical(reason: str):\n    return technical_agent\n\n# Add tools to triage agent\ntriage_agent.functions = [route_to_billing, route_to_technical]\n\n# Execute swarm\nclient = Swarm()\nresponse = client.run(\n    agent=triage_agent,\n    messages=[{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"I have a billing question\"}]\n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Orchestration Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Sequential Task Chain\n\nAgents execute tasks in sequence, each building on previous results:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Task 1: Research\nresearch_output = research_agent.work(\"Analyze AI market trends\")\n\n# Task 2: Analysis (uses research output)\nanalysis = analyst_agent.work(f\"Analyze these findings: {research_output}\")\n\n# Task 3: Report (uses analysis)\nreport = writer_agent.work(f\"Write report on: {analysis}\")\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen to Use: Steps have dependencies, each builds on previous\n\n### Pattern 2: Parallel Execution\n\nMultiple agents work simultaneously, results combined:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nimport asyncio\n\nasync def parallel_teams():\n    # All agents work in parallel\n    market_task = market_agent.work_async(\"Analyze market\")\n    technical_task = tech_agent.work_async(\"Analyze technology\")\n    user_task = user_agent.work_async(\"Analyze user needs\")\n\n    # Wait for all to complete\n    market_results, tech_results, user_results = await asyncio.gather(\n        market_task, technical_task, user_task\n    )\n\n    # Synthesize results\n    return synthesize(market_results, tech_results, user_results)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen to Use: Independent analyses, need quick results, want diversity\n\n### Pattern 3: Hierarchical Structure\n\nManager agent coordinates specialists:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nmanager_agent.orchestrate({\n    \"market_analysis\": {\n        \"agents\": [competitor_analyst, trend_analyst],\n        \"task\": \"Comprehensive market analysis\"\n    },\n    \"technical_evaluation\": {\n        \"agents\": [architecture_agent, security_agent],\n        \"task\": \"Technical feasibility assessment\"\n    },\n    \"synthesis\": {\n        \"agents\": [strategy_agent],\n        \"task\": \"Create strategic recommendations\"\n    }\n})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen to Use: Clear hierarchy, different teams, complex coordination\n\n### Pattern 4: Debate &amp; Consensus\n\nMultiple agents discuss and reach consensus:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nagents = [bull_agent, bear_agent, neutral_agent]\nquestion = \"Should we invest in this startup?\"\n\n# Debate round 1\narguments = {agent: agent.argue(question) for agent in agents}\n\n# Debate round 2 (respond to others)\ncounter_arguments = {\n    agent: agent.respond(arguments) for agent in agents\n}\n\n# Reach consensus\nconsensus = mediator_agent.synthesize_consensus(counter_arguments)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen to Use: Complex decisions, need multiple perspectives, risk assessment\n\n## Agent Communication Patterns\n\n### 1. Direct Communication\n\nAgents pass messages directly to each other:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nagent_a.send_message(agent_b, {\n    \"type\": \"request\",\n    \"action\": \"analyze_document\",\n    \"document\": doc_content,\n    \"context\": {\"deadline\": \"urgent\"}\n})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Tool-Mediated Communication\n\nAgents use shared tools/databases:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Agent A writes to shared memory\nshared_memory.write(\"findings\", {\"market_size\": \"$5B\", \"growth\": \"20%\"})\n\n# Agent B reads from shared memory\nfindings = shared_memory.read(\"findings\")\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 3. Manager-Based Communication\n\nCentral coordinator manages agent communication:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nmanager.broadcast(\"update_all_agents\", {\n    \"new_deadline\": \"tomorrow\",\n    \"priority\": \"critical\"\n})\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### Agent Design\n\n- ✓ Reasonable scope of work\n- ✓ Distinct from other agents\n- ✓ Relevant background/expertise\n- ✓ Appropriate tools for the role\n- ✓ Clear, specific role and goal\n\n### Workflow Design\n\n- ✓ Performance monitoring\n- ✓ Fallback strategies\n- ✓ Error handling between agents\n- ✓ Identified handoff points\n- ✓ Clear task dependencies\n\n### Communication\n\n- ✓ Audit logging\n- ✓ Timeout handling\n- ✓ Error propagation strategy\n- ✓ Clear context sharing\n- ✓ Structured message formats\n\n### Orchestration\n\n- ✓ Allow human intervention points\n- ✓ Implement feedback loops\n- ✓ Monitor agent performance\n- ✓ Set clear success criteria\n- ✓ Define process clearly (sequential, parallel, etc.)\n\n## Common Challenges &amp; Solutions\n\n### Challenge: Agent Conflicts\n\nSolutions:\n\n- Clear authority hierarchy\n- Conflict resolution agent\n- Consensus mechanisms\n- Explicit decision-making rules\n- Clear role separation\n\n### Challenge: Slow Execution\n\nSolutions:\n\n- Implement timeout handling\n- Optimize agent logic\n- Pre-process data\n- Cache results from expensive operations\n- Use parallel execution where possible\n\n### Challenge: Poor Quality Results\n\nSolutions:\n\n- Result aggregation strategies\n- Quality validation agents\n- Feedback integration\n- More relevant tools\n- Better agent prompts/instructions\n\n### Challenge: Complex Workflows\n\nSolutions:\n\n- Documentation of workflow\n- Good state management\n- Clear task definitions\n- Hierarchical structure\n- Break into smaller teams\n\n## Evaluation Metrics\n\nTeam Performance:\n\n- Error rate\n- Cost (tokens/API calls)\n- Execution time\n- Quality of results\n- Task completion rate\n\nAgent Effectiveness:\n\n- Collaboration score\n- Communication clarity\n- Tool usage efficiency\n- Response quality\n- Task success rate\n\n## Advanced Techniques\n\n### 1. Self-Organizing Teams\n\nAgents autonomously decide roles and workflow:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Agents negotiate roles based on task\nagents = [agent1, agent2, agent3]\ntask = \"complex financial analysis\"\n\n# Agents determine best structure\nnegotiated_structure = self_organize(agents, task)\n# Returns optimal workflow for this task\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Adaptive Workflows\n\nWorkflow changes based on progress:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Monitor progress\nif progress &lt; expected_rate:\n    # Increase resources\n    workflow.add_agent(specialist_agent)\nelif quality &lt; threshold:\n    # Increase validation\n    workflow.insert_review_step()\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 3. Cross-Agent Learning\n\nAgents learn from each other's work:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# After team execution\nexecution_trace = crew.get_execution_trace()\n\n# Extract learnings\nlearnings = extract_patterns(execution_trace)\n\n# Update agent knowledge\nfor agent, learning in learnings.items():\n    agent.update_knowledge(learning)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Resources\n\n### Frameworks\n\n- Swarm: [https://github.com/openai/swarm](https://github.com/openai/swarm)\n- LangGraph: [https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/](https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/)\n- AutoGen: [https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/)\n- CrewAI: [https://crewai.com/](https://crewai.com/)\n\n### Papers\n\n- \"Self-Organizing Multi-Agent Systems\" (research papers)\n- \"Generative Agents\" (Park et al.)\n\n## Implementation Checklist\n\n- Document workflow and decisions\n- Optimize based on results\n- Evaluate quality and performance\n- Test team collaboration\n- Implement monitoring/logging\n- Add error handling and fallbacks\n- Set success criteria for each task\n- Implement task definitions\n- Define communication patterns\n- Plan workflow (sequential, parallel, hierarchical)\n- Identify available tools/capabilities for each agent\n- Define each agent's role, goal, and expertise\n\n## Getting Started\n\n1. Scale: Add agents and complexity as needed\n2. Iterate: Refine based on results\n3. Monitor Closely: Track performance and results\n4. Test Thoroughly: Validate agent behavior individually and together\n5. Clear Workflow: Document how agents interact\n6. →&#96;senior-engineer&#96;,&#96;developer&#96;,&#96;senior-dev&#96;\n\n## Task Routing\n\nRoute by role, then select the best available agent for that role:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nUser request    │\n    ├─► \"deploy/configure/CI/design/a11y\" ──► specialist role\n    │\n    └─► \"capture this workflow/make reusable\" ──► patterns role\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Workflow Patterns\n\n### Document trade-offs — When choosing between options, record why\n2. Consider backward compatibility — Breaking changes require explicit approval\n3. Optimize for maintainability — Next developer (or agent) must understand it\n4. Prefer simplicity — Cleverness is a liability; simple is maintainable\n5. Favor existing patterns — Match what's already in the codebase\n\nThese principles apply across all roles. Agents should surface decisions to the orchestrator when trade-offs are significant.\n\n## Communication Style\n\nOrchestrators and agents should:\n\n- Include file paths and line numbers when referencing code\n- Provide clear summaries of delegated work (what was done, what remains)\n- Flag blockers immediately — don't spin on unsolvable problems\n- Report progress at each major step (don't go silent)\n\nProgress format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\n░░░░░░░░░░ [1/5] research: Exploring auth patterns\n▓▓▓▓░░░░░░ [2/5] coding: Implementing refresh token flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## When to Escalate\n\n- Missing context: Not enough info → research role with pathfinding skill\n- Scope creep: Task expands beyond role's domain → re-route\n- Conflicting findings: Multiple agents disagree → surface to user\n- Blocked: Agent can't proceed → route to research role\n\n## Anti-Patterns\n\n- Not challenging complex proposals (over-engineering)\n- Parallel agents with dependencies (race conditions)\n- Coding role debugging without debugging skills (inefficient)\n- Skipping reviewing role for \"small changes\" (risk)\n- Running all agents on every task (wasteful)\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n\"I need to build X\" → coding role + TDD skills\n\n\"Review this PR\" → reviewing role + code-review\n\n\"Why is this broken?\" → debugging role + debugging-and-diagnosis\n\n\"Is this approach overkill?\" → challenging role + complexity-analysis\n\n\"Prove this works\" → testing role + scenario-testing\n\n\"What's the codebase doing?\" → research role + codebase-analysis\n\n\"Deploy to production\" → specialist role + domain skills\n\n\"Make this workflow reusable\" → patterns role + patternify\n\nWeekly Installs\n\n6\n\nRepository\n\n[outfitter-dev/agents](https://github.com/outfitter-dev/agents)\n\nGitHub Stars\n\n24\n\nFirst Seen\n\nJan 22, 2026\n\nInstalled on\n\nclaude-code5\n\nwindsurf3\n\nopencode3\n\ncursor3\n\ncodex3\n\nantigravity3"
```json
}
```

</tool-use>

Monitor execution: Track agent activities\n2. Create supervisor: Orchestrate execution\n3. Add shared memory: Persistent context\n4. Implement handoffs: Agent-to-agent communication\n5. Design workflow: Plan agent coordination\n6. Define agents: Create specialized agents\n\n## Agent Architecture\n\n### Agent Definition\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// agents/base.ts\nimport { ChatOpenAI } from '@langchain/openai';\nimport { SystemMessage, HumanMessage, AIMessage } from '@langchain/core/messages';\n\nexport interface AgentConfig {\n  name: string;\n  role: string;\n  systemPrompt: string;\n  tools?: Tool[];\n  model?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface AgentResponse {\n  content: string;\n  toolCalls?: ToolCall[];\n  nextAgent?: string;\n  completed?: boolean;\n}\n\nexport class Agent {\n  private model: ChatOpenAI;\n  private config: AgentConfig;\n  private messageHistory: BaseMessage[] = [];\n\n  constructor(config: AgentConfig) {\n    this.config = config;\n    this.model = new ChatOpenAI({\n      modelName: config.model || 'gpt-4-turbo-preview',\n      temperature: 0.7,\n    });\n  }\n\n  async execute(input: string, context?: Record&lt;string, any): Promise&lt;AgentResponse {\n    const systemMessage = new SystemMessage(\n      this.buildSystemPrompt(context)\n    );\n\n    const messages = [\n      systemMessage,\n      ...this.messageHistory,\n      new HumanMessage(input),\n    ];\n\n    const response = await this.model.invoke(messages, {\n      tools: this.config.tools,\n    });\n\n    this.messageHistory.push(new HumanMessage(input));\n    this.messageHistory.push(new AIMessage(response.content as string));\n\n    return this.parseResponse(response);\n  }\n\n  private buildSystemPrompt(context?: Record&lt;string, any): string {\n    let prompt = this.config.systemPrompt;\n\n    if (context) {\n      prompt += &#96;\\n\\nContext:\\n${JSON.stringify(context, null, 2)}&#96;;\n    }\n\n    return prompt;\n  }\n\n  private parseResponse(response: any): AgentResponse {\n    // Parse tool calls and determine next actions\n    return {\n      content: response.content as string,\n      toolCalls: response.tool_calls,\n      completed: response.content?.includes('[TASK_COMPLETE]'),\n    };\n  }\n\n  clearHistory() {\n    this.messageHistory = [];\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Specialized Agents\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// agents/specialists.ts\nimport { Agent, AgentConfig } from './base';\n\nexport const ResearchAgent = new Agent({\n  name: 'researcher',\n  role: 'Research Specialist',\n  systemPrompt: &#96;You are a research specialist. Your job is to:\n- Search for and gather relevant information\n- Analyze sources and extract key insights\n- Summarize findings clearly\n- Cite sources when possible\n\nWhen you have gathered sufficient information, include [TASK_COMPLETE] in your response.\nIf you need help from another agent, specify: [HANDOFF:agent_name]&#96;,\n  tools: [searchTool, webScrapeTool],\n});\n\nexport const WriterAgent = new Agent({\n  name: 'writer',\n  role: 'Content Writer',\n  systemPrompt: &#96;You are a professional content writer. Your job is to:\n- Create engaging, well-structured content\n- Adapt tone and style to the target audience\n- Incorporate research and data effectively\n- Edit and refine for clarity\n\nUse the research provided to create compelling content.\nWhen complete, include [TASK_COMPLETE].&#96;,\n});\n\nexport const ReviewerAgent = new Agent({\n  name: 'reviewer',\n  role: 'Quality Reviewer',\n  systemPrompt: &#96;You are a quality reviewer. Your job is to:\n- Review content for accuracy and clarity\n- Check for errors and inconsistencies\n- Suggest improvements\n- Approve or request revisions\n\nProvide specific feedback. If approved, include [APPROVED].\nIf revisions needed, include [REVISIONS_NEEDED] with specific changes.&#96;,\n});\n\nexport const PlannerAgent = new Agent({\n  name: 'planner',\n  role: 'Task Planner',\n  systemPrompt: &#96;You are a task planner. Your job is to:\n- Break down complex tasks into subtasks\n- Identify which specialist agent should handle each subtask\n- Create an execution order\n- Track progress\n\nOutput a structured plan in JSON format:\n{\n  \"goal\": \"...\",\n  \"steps\": [\n    { \"step\": 1, \"agent\": \"researcher\", \"task\": \"...\" },\n    { \"step\": 2, \"agent\": \"writer\", \"task\": \"...\" }\n  ]\n}&#96;,\n});\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Orchestrator\n\n### Simple Sequential Orchestrator\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// orchestrator/sequential.ts\nimport { Agent } from '../agents/base';\n\ninterface WorkflowStep {\n  agent: Agent;\n  task: string;\n  inputFrom?: string;\n}\n\nexport class SequentialOrchestrator {\n  private agents: Map&lt;string, Agent = new Map();\n  private results: Map&lt;string, string = new Map();\n\n  registerAgent(name: string, agent: Agent) {\n    this.agents.set(name, agent);\n  }\n\n  async execute(workflow: WorkflowStep[]): Promise&lt;Record&lt;string, string {\n    for (const step of workflow) {\n      const agent = step.agent;\n\n      // Get input from previous step if specified\n      let input = step.task;\n      if (step.inputFrom &amp;&amp; this.results.has(step.inputFrom)) {\n        input = &#96;${step.task}\\n\\nPrevious output:\\n${this.results.get(step.inputFrom)}&#96;;\n      }\n\n      console.log(&#96;Executing: ${agent.name} - ${step.task}&#96;);\n\n      const result = await agent.execute(input);\n      this.results.set(agent.name, result.content);\n\n      console.log(&#96;Completed: ${agent.name}&#96;);\n    }\n\n    return Object.fromEntries(this.results);\n  }\n}\n\n// Usage\nconst orchestrator = new SequentialOrchestrator();\norchestrator.registerAgent('researcher', ResearchAgent);\norchestrator.registerAgent('writer', WriterAgent);\norchestrator.registerAgent('reviewer', ReviewerAgent);\n\nconst results = await orchestrator.execute([\n  { agent: ResearchAgent, task: 'Research the latest AI trends in 2024' },\n  { agent: WriterAgent, task: 'Write a blog post about AI trends', inputFrom: 'researcher' },\n  { agent: ReviewerAgent, task: 'Review the blog post', inputFrom: 'writer' },\n]);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Supervisor Orchestrator\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// orchestrator/supervisor.ts\nimport { ChatOpenAI } from '@langchain/openai';\nimport { Agent } from '../agents/base';\n\ninterface AgentRegistry {\n  [name: string]: {\n    agent: Agent;\n    description: string;\n  };\n}\n\nexport class SupervisorOrchestrator {\n  private supervisor: ChatOpenAI;\n  private agents: AgentRegistry = {};\n  private sharedContext: Record&lt;string, any = {};\n  private maxIterations = 10;\n\n  constructor() {\n    this.supervisor = new ChatOpenAI({\n      modelName: 'gpt-4-turbo-preview',\n      temperature: 0,\n    });\n  }\n\n  registerAgent(name: string, agent: Agent, description: string) {\n    this.agents[name] = { agent, description };\n  }\n\n  async execute(task: string): Promise&lt;string {\n    let iteration = 0;\n    let currentTask = task;\n    const history: string[] = [];\n\n    while (iteration &lt; this.maxIterations) {\n      iteration++;\n\n      // Supervisor decides next action\n      const decision = await this.supervise(currentTask, history);\n\n      if (decision.complete) {\n        return decision.finalResponse!;\n      }\n\n      // Execute selected agent\n      const { agent } = this.agents[decision.nextAgent!];\n      const result = await agent.execute(decision.agentTask!, this.sharedContext);\n\n      // Update shared context\n      this.sharedContext[decision.nextAgent!] = result.content;\n      history.push(&#96;${decision.nextAgent}: ${result.content}&#96;);\n\n      // Check for handoff\n      if (result.nextAgent) {\n        currentTask = &#96;Continue with: ${result.content}&#96;;\n      }\n    }\n\n    throw new Error('Max iterations reached');\n  }\n\n  private async supervise(\n    task: string,\n    history: string[]\n  ): Promise&lt;{\n    complete: boolean;\n    finalResponse?: string;\n    nextAgent?: string;\n    agentTask?: string;\n  } {\n    const agentList = Object.entries(this.agents)\n      .map(([name, { description }]) = &#96;- ${name}: ${description}&#96;)\n      .join('\\n');\n\n    const prompt = &#96;You are a supervisor coordinating AI agents.\n\nAvailable agents:\n${agentList}\n\nTask: ${task}\n\nHistory:\n${history.join('\\n')}\n\nDecide the next action. Respond in JSON:\n{\n  \"thought\": \"your reasoning\",\n  \"complete\": false,\n  \"nextAgent\": \"agent_name\",\n  \"agentTask\": \"specific task for the agent\"\n}\n\nOr if the task is complete:\n{\n  \"thought\": \"your reasoning\",\n  \"complete\": true,\n  \"finalResponse\": \"the final answer\"\n}&#96;;\n\n    const response = await this.supervisor.invoke([{ role: 'user', content: prompt }]);\n    return JSON.parse(response.content as string);\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Parallel Agent Execution\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// orchestrator/parallel.ts\nexport class ParallelOrchestrator {\n  private agents: Map&lt;string, Agent = new Map();\n\n  async executeParallel(\n    tasks: Array&lt;{ agentName: string; task: string }\n  ): Promise&lt;Map&lt;string, string {\n    const results = new Map&lt;string, string();\n\n    await Promise.all(\n      tasks.map(async ({ agentName, task }) = {\n        const agent = this.agents.get(agentName);\n        if (!agent) throw new Error(&#96;Agent ${agentName} not found&#96;);\n\n        const result = await agent.execute(task);\n        results.set(agentName, result.content);\n      })\n    );\n\n    return results;\n  }\n\n  async fanOutFanIn(\n    task: string,\n    agentNames: string[],\n    aggregator: Agent\n  ): Promise&lt;string {\n    // Fan out: same task to multiple agents\n    const parallelResults = await this.executeParallel(\n      agentNames.map((name) = ({ agentName: name, task }))\n    );\n\n    // Fan in: aggregate results\n    const aggregatedInput = Array.from(parallelResults.entries())\n      .map(([name, result]) = &#96;${name}:\\n${result}&#96;)\n      .join('\\n\\n---\\n\\n');\n\n    const finalResult = await aggregator.execute(\n      &#96;Synthesize these perspectives:\\n\\n${aggregatedInput}&#96;\n    );\n\n    return finalResult.content;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Shared Memory\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// memory/shared.ts\nimport { Redis } from 'ioredis';\n\nexport class SharedMemory {\n  private redis: Redis;\n  private prefix: string;\n\n  constructor(sessionId: string) {\n    this.redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!);\n    this.prefix = &#96;agent:${sessionId}:&#96;;\n  }\n\n  async set(key: string, value: any, ttl?: number): Promise&lt;void {\n    const serialized = JSON.stringify(value);\n    if (ttl) {\n      await this.redis.setex(this.prefix + key, ttl, serialized);\n    } else {\n      await this.redis.set(this.prefix + key, serialized);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async get&lt;T(key: string): Promise&lt;T | null {\n    const value = await this.redis.get(this.prefix + key);\n    return value ? JSON.parse(value) : null;\n  }\n\n  async append(key: string, item: any): Promise&lt;void {\n    const list = (await this.get&lt;any[](key)) || [];\n    list.push(item);\n    await this.set(key, list);\n  }\n\n  async getConversation(): Promise&lt;Message[] {\n    return (await this.get&lt;Message[]('conversation')) || [];\n  }\n\n  async addMessage(message: Message): Promise&lt;void {\n    await this.append('conversation', message);\n  }\n\n  async getAgentOutputs(): Promise&lt;Record&lt;string, string {\n    return (await this.get&lt;Record&lt;string, string('outputs')) || {};\n  }\n\n  async setAgentOutput(agent: string, output: string): Promise&lt;void {\n    const outputs = await this.getAgentOutputs();\n    outputs[agent] = output;\n    await this.set('outputs', outputs);\n  }\n\n  async clear(): Promise&lt;void {\n    const keys = await this.redis.keys(this.prefix + '*');\n    if (keys.length  0) {\n      await this.redis.del(...keys);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Event-Driven Agent Communication\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// events/agent-events.ts\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\n\nexport class AgentEventBus extends EventEmitter {\n  private static instance: AgentEventBus;\n\n  static getInstance(): AgentEventBus {\n    if (!this.instance) {\n      this.instance = new AgentEventBus();\n    }\n    return this.instance;\n  }\n\n  emitAgentMessage(from: string, to: string, message: any) {\n    this.emit(&#96;message:${to}&#96;, { from, message, timestamp: new Date() });\n  }\n\n  emitAgentComplete(agent: string, result: any) {\n    this.emit('agent:complete', { agent, result, timestamp: new Date() });\n  }\n\n  emitAgentError(agent: string, error: Error) {\n    this.emit('agent:error', { agent, error, timestamp: new Date() });\n  }\n\n  onMessage(agentName: string, handler: (message: any) = void) {\n    this.on(&#96;message:${agentName}&#96;, handler);\n  }\n\n  onAnyComplete(handler: (event: any) = void) {\n    this.on('agent:complete', handler);\n  }\n}\n\n// Usage\nconst eventBus = AgentEventBus.getInstance();\n\n// Agent listens for messages\neventBus.onMessage('writer', async ({ from, message }) = {\n  console.log(&#96;Writer received from ${from}:&#96;, message);\n  const result = await WriterAgent.execute(message);\n  eventBus.emitAgentComplete('writer', result);\n});\n\n// Orchestrator listens for completions\neventBus.onAnyComplete(({ agent, result }) = {\n  console.log(&#96;${agent} completed:&#96;, result.content);\n});\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Workflow Definition\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// workflows/definition.ts\ninterface WorkflowDefinition {\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  agents: string[];\n  steps: WorkflowStep[];\n  errorHandling: 'retry' | 'fallback' | 'abort';\n}\n\nconst ContentCreationWorkflow: WorkflowDefinition = {\n  name: 'content-creation',\n  description: 'Create and publish content',\n  agents: ['planner', 'researcher', 'writer', 'reviewer'],\n  steps: [\n    {\n      id: 'plan',\n      agent: 'planner',\n      input: '{task}',\n      outputKey: 'plan',\n    },\n    {\n      id: 'research',\n      agent: 'researcher',\n      input: 'Research for: {plan.topic}',\n      outputKey: 'research',\n      parallel: true,\n    },\n    {\n      id: 'write',\n      agent: 'writer',\n      input: 'Write about {plan.topic} using research: {research}',\n      outputKey: 'draft',\n      dependsOn: ['research'],\n    },\n    {\n      id: 'review',\n      agent: 'reviewer',\n      input: 'Review: {draft}',\n      outputKey: 'review',\n      loop: {\n        condition: 'review.approved === false',\n        maxIterations: 3,\n        backTo: 'write',\n      },\n    },\n  ],\n  errorHandling: 'retry',\n};\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Best Practices\n\n1. Timeout handling: Prevent stuck agents\n2. Testing: Test agents individually and together\n3. Observability: Log all agent actions\n4. Error handling: Graceful degradation\n5. Iteration limits: Prevent infinite loops\n6. Shared context: Use memory for persistent state\n7. Clear handoffs: Explicit agent-to-agent communication\n8. Single responsibility: Each agent has one clear role\n\n## Output Checklist\n\nEvery agent system should include:\n\n- Agent handoff protocol\n- Logging and monitoring\n- Iteration limits\n- Error handling and retries\n- Workflow definitions\n- Event-based communication\n- Shared memory system\n- Orchestrator for coordination\n- Specialized agents with clear roles\n- Agent base class with common functionality\n\nWeekly Installs\n\n48\n\nRepository\n\n[patricio0312rev/skills](https://github.com/patricio0312rev/skills)\n\nGitHub Stars\n\n16\n\nFirst Seen\n\nJan 24, 2026\n\nSecurity Audits\n\n[Gen Agent Trust HubPass](https://skills.sh/patricio0312rev/skills/ai-agent-orchestrator/security/agent-trust-hub)\n\n[SocketPass](https://skills.sh/patricio0312rev/skills/ai-agent-orchestrator/security/socket)\n\n[SnykWarn](https://skills.sh/patricio0312rev/skills/ai-agent-orchestrator/security/snyk)\n\nInstalled on\n\ncodex42\n\nopencode42\n\ngithub-copilot40\n\ngemini-cli39\n\nclaude-code35\n\ncursor34"
```json
}
```

```json
  "markdown": "# Gist: Claude Code Swarm Orchestration Skill - Complete guide to multi-agent coordination with TeammateTool, Task system, and all patterns\n\n- Author: [@kieranklaassen](https://github.com/kieranklaassen)\n- Public gist (1 file)\n- Created: 2026-01-25T23:54:02Z\n- Updated: 2026-03-13T13:30:23Z\n- Forks: 87\n\n## SKILL.md\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;md\n---\nname: orchestrating-swarms\ndescription: Master multi-agent orchestration using Claude Code's TeammateTool and Task system. Use when coordinating multiple agents, running parallel code reviews, creating pipeline workflows with dependencies, building self-organizing task queues, or any task benefiting from divide-and-conquer patterns.\n---\n\n# Claude Code Swarm Orchestration\n\nMaster multi-agent orchestration using Claude Code's TeammateTool and Task system.\n\n---\n\n## Primitives\n\n| Primitive | What It Is | File Location |\n|-----------|-----------|---------------|\n| **Agent** | A Claude instance that can use tools. You are an agent. Subagents are agents you spawn. | N/A (process) |\n| **Team** | A named group of agents working together. One leader, multiple teammates. | &#96;~/.claude/teams/{name}/config.json&#96; |\n| **Teammate** | An agent that joined a team. Has a name, color, inbox. Spawned via Task with &#96;team_name&#96; + &#96;name&#96;. | Listed in team config |\n| **Leader** | The agent that created the team. Receives teammate messages, approves plans/shutdowns. | First member in config |\n| **Task** | A work item with subject, description, status, owner, and dependencies. | &#96;~/.claude/tasks/{team}/N.json&#96; |\n| **Inbox** | JSON file where an agent receives messages from teammates. | &#96;~/.claude/teams/{name}/inboxes/{agent}.json&#96; |\n| **Message** | A JSON object sent between agents. Can be text or structured (shutdown_request, idle_notification, etc). | Stored in inbox files |\n| **Backend** | How teammates run. See [Spawn Backends](#spawn-backends). | Auto-detected based on environment |\n\n### How They Connect\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph TEAM[TEAM]\n        Leader[Leader - you]\n        T1[Teammate 1]\n        T2[Teammate 2]\n\n        Leader &lt;--&gt;|messages via inbox| T1\n        Leader &lt;--&gt;|messages via inbox| T2\n        T1 &lt;-.-&gt;|can message| T2\n    end\n\n    subgraph TASKS[TASK LIST]\n        Task1[\"#1 completed: Research&lt;br/&gt;owner: teammate1\"]\n        Task2[\"#2 in_progress: Implement&lt;br/&gt;owner: teammate2\"]\n        Task3[\"#3 pending: Test&lt;br/&gt;blocked by #2\"]\n    end\n\n    T1 --&gt; Task1\n    T2 --&gt; Task2\n    Task2 -.-&gt;|unblocks| Task3\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Lifecycle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    A[1. Create Team] --&gt; B[2. Create Tasks]\n    B --&gt; C[3. Spawn Teammates]\n    C --&gt; D[4. Work]\n    D --&gt; E[5. Coordinate]\n    E --&gt; F[6. Shutdown]\n    F --&gt; G[7. Cleanup]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Message Flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant L as Leader\n    participant T1 as Teammate 1\n    participant T2 as Teammate 2\n    participant Tasks as Task List\n\n    L-&gt;&gt;Tasks: TaskCreate (3 tasks)\n    L-&gt;&gt;T1: spawn with prompt\n    L-&gt;&gt;T2: spawn with prompt\n\n    T1-&gt;&gt;Tasks: claim task #1\n    T2-&gt;&gt;Tasks: claim task #2\n\n    T1-&gt;&gt;Tasks: complete #1\n    T1-&gt;&gt;L: send findings (inbox)\n\n    Note over Tasks: #3 auto-unblocks\n\n    T2-&gt;&gt;Tasks: complete #2\n    T2-&gt;&gt;L: send findings (inbox)\n\n    L-&gt;&gt;T1: requestShutdown\n    T1-&gt;&gt;L: approveShutdown\n    L-&gt;&gt;T2: requestShutdown\n    T2-&gt;&gt;L: approveShutdown\n\n    L-&gt;&gt;L: cleanup\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1.
```
